duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: Genesis depositors steal accumulated gauge rewards because depositsForGenesis calls updateReward(address(0)) instead of updateReward(_tokenOwner), leaving userRewardPerTokenPaid at 0
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All seven hypotheses describe the identical bug: updateReward(address(0)) in _depositsForGenesis skips user checkpoint, enabling retroactive reward theft. TECH-H001 has the most comprehensive technical analysis with specific line references and exploit mechanics.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: swapExactTokensForTokensSimple is completely non-functional because routes[0].pair and routes[0].receiver are never initialized, defaulting to address(0)
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All describe the same bug: incomplete route struct initialization in swapExactTokensForTokensSimple omitting pair and receiver fields. Note: TECH2-H003 has duplicate IDs in the input; the instance titled 'Uninitialized Route Structs in swapExactTokensForTokensSimple' belongs to this group.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: _swapSupportingFeeOnTransferTokens uses getAmountOut which returns best of stable/volatile, but executes swap on the specific pool type in routes[i].stable, causing K-invariant failures
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All describe the pool type mismatch between amount calculation and swap execution in FoT swap paths. Note: TECH2-H007 here refers to the instance titled 'Inconsistent Pool Selection in Fee-on-Transfer Swaps'.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: TradeHelper._pairFor uses wrong CREATE2 deployer (PairFactory instead of PairGenerator) and incomplete init code hash, making GlobalRouter completely broken
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All trace to the same root cause: TradeHelper._pairFor computes incorrect CREATE2 addresses. Some focus on TradeHelper specifically, others on GlobalRouter impact, but the underlying defect is identical.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: RouterV2._swap ignores its _to parameter entirely, using routes[i].receiver for all output routing, breaking swapExactTokensForETH and violating API contract
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All describe the same dead-code bug: _to parameter in _swap is never referenced, routes[i].receiver controls all destinations. TECH-H005 provides the most complete analysis of the swapExactTokensForETH failure path.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: TradeHelper.getAmountsIn has an infinite loop / immediate revert due to unsigned integer underflow when decrementing loop counter past 0
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All describe the identical uint underflow bug in getAmountsIn's for loop. Note: TECH2-H006 here refers to the instance titled 'Infinite Loop in TradeHelper getAmountsIn'.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: TradeHelper.getAmountInStable uses y_1 = reserveOut + output (should be minus) and wrong decimal conversion, returning completely incorrect values
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All describe the same two math errors in getAmountInStable: inverted formula direction and wrong decimal conversion. Note: TECH2-H008 here refers to the instance titled 'Incorrect Math in TradeHelper getAmountInStable'.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: GlobalRouter.exactInput function body is entirely commented out, silently returning 0 and potentially trapping ETH
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All describe the same no-op function. OPS-H005 additionally identifies the ETH trapping risk via the payable modifier.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: GaugeExtraRewarder uses TOKEN.balanceOf(GAUGE) instead of gauge._totalSupply for reward calculations, enabling dilution via direct LP token donations
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All describe the same balanceOf vs _totalSupply mismatch in GaugeExtraRewarder's lpSupply calculation and the resulting reward dilution attack vector.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: Pair.claimStakingFees is permissionless and sends fees to PairFactory.stakingFeeHandler which may be unset (address(0)), burning accumulated fees
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: Both describe the same issue: permissionless claimStakingFees with no zero-address check on stakingFeeHandler. TECH-H013 provides more comprehensive analysis.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: Pair TWAP oracle accumulates time-weighted reserves instead of time-weighted prices, making it mathematically incorrect and more manipulable
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All describe the same oracle design flaw: reserve0CumulativeLast += reserve * timeElapsed instead of price * timeElapsed. ECON-H017 has the best economic analysis of manipulation costs.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: removeLiquidityETHSupportingFeeOnTransferTokens sweeps router's entire token balance instead of just the amount from liquidity removal
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: Both describe the same balance sweep bug using balanceOf(address(this)) instead of amountToken. ECON-H018 has better attack economics analysis.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: Pair.mint and Pair.burn lack isPaused check while swap has it, enabling liquidity manipulation during emergency pause
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All describe the asymmetric pause mechanism where swap checks isPaused but mint/burn do not. Note: TECH2-H010 here refers to the instance titled 'Asymmetric Pause Mechanism Allows Liquidity Manipulation'.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: CustomPoolDeployer.createCustomPool lacks access control (unlike other privileged functions that use onlyAuthorized), allowing arbitrary pool creation and registry pollution
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: Both describe the same missing access control on createCustomPool. Note: TECH2-H008 here refers to the instance titled 'CustomPoolDeployer createCustomPool is permissionless'.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: RouterV2.swapExactTokensForTokens sends tokens to user-controlled routes[0].pair but executes swap on computed pairFor() address, enabling extraction of excess pair balances
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All describe the same split between transfer destination (routes[0].pair, user-controlled) and swap execution target (pairFor, computed). ECON-H012 has the most detailed attack economics.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: PairFactory.setReferralFee has no upper bound validation; setting > 10000 causes arithmetic underflow in _update0/_update1, DoSing all swaps
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: Both describe the identical unbounded referral fee vulnerability. OPS-H006 provides more detailed analysis of the underflow mechanics.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: GaugeFactory.createGauge is permissionless, allowing arbitrary gauge deployment that can pollute registries and confuse UIs/admins
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All describe the same missing access control on createGauge. OPS-H014 has the most comprehensive analysis of downstream impact.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: Fee accumulator precision loss in Pair._update0/_update1 where amount * 1e18 / totalSupply rounds to 0 for small fees, permanently locking dust in PairFees
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: Both describe the same rounding-to-zero issue in fee index calculation. Note: TECH2-H009 here refers to the instance titled 'Precision Loss in Fee Accumulator Causes Dust Fee Loss for LPs'.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: GaugeExtraRewarder._pendingReward can underflow when computed accrued amount is less than rewardDebt due to rounding, blocking gauge operations via callback revert
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: Both describe the same underflow risk in pending reward calculation. Note: TECH2-H010 here refers to the instance titled 'GaugeExtraRewarder pending reward calculation may underflow'.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: WAVAX.withdraw uses payable(msg.sender).transfer(wad) with 2300 gas stipend, preventing smart contracts with complex receive functions from withdrawing
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: Both describe the same 2300 gas limitation in WAVAX.withdraw. Note: TECH2-H009 here refers to the instance titled 'WAVAX withdraw uses 2300-gas transfer'. OPS2-H008 provides clearer impact analysis.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: UNSAFE_swapExactTokensForTokens accepts user-supplied amounts[] without validation, enabling extraction of excess pair balances or router balances through crafted swap parameters
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All target UNSAFE_swapExactTokensForTokens's lack of amount validation. Note: TECH2-H003 here refers to the instance titled 'RouterV2 permissionless UNSAFE_swapExactTokensForTokens'. ECON-H014 identifies the most severe attack vector (CL path draining router balance).

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: GaugeV2 withdraw/emergencyWithdraw paths lack explicit amount <= _balanceOf check, relying on _deductBalance which can desync _totalSupply if genesis pool behaves inconsistently
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: Both describe the same root cause: _totalSupply decremented before _deductBalance validates the amount against the user's combined balance (gauge + genesis pool). ECON2-H016 provides more detailed analysis of the genesis pool desync risk.
